title: Bridge Essay: Tracking the neurodynamics of insight: A meta-analysis of neuroimaging studies.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight.
**Phase context:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight, involuntary cognition.

**Tracking the neurodynamics of insight: A meta-analysis of neuroimaging studies.** — Biol Psychol (2018). DOI: 10.1016/j.biopsycho.2018.08.018. PMID: 30165082. The nature of insight has been the interdisciplinary focus of scientific inquiry for over 100 years. Behavioral studies and biographical data suggest that insight, as a form of creative cognition, consists of at least four separate but intercorrelated stages as described by Wallas (1926). Yet no quantitative evidence was available for insight- or insight-stage-specific brain mechanisms that generalize across various insight tasks.
